description_long: This is a robust, arched entry gate set within thick, rectangular pillars, likely constructed from concrete or stone. The archway is weathered, with visible stains and discoloration, and features faint, partially legible text across the top. Two lantern-style lamps are mounted atop the pillars on either side, providing illumination and a decorative touch. The gate itself consists of two metal swinging doors, each with a simple, utilitarian design. The base of the structure shows signs of dirt and wear, suggesting outdoor placement and exposure to the elements. The overall appearance is utilitarian with minimal ornamentation, suitable for an industrial, historical, or institutional urban setting.
description: A weathered arched gate with thick pillars, metal swing doors, and lanterns on each side, showing signs of outdoor use and minimal decoration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched entry, two lanterns, and the double metal gate doors with visible weathering and partial text.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the pillars and the profile of the arch, with one lantern visible.
description_view_2: Rear view of the gate, showing the arch and pillars from behind, with both lanterns and the back of the metal doors visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the pillar thickness and lantern from the other side.
